t_en: Oh, it's nothing, I was here anyway...

t_ch: 那就是命运使然——看来，这幅画像注定需要你的意见才能彻底完成……
t_en: That is fate, then — it seems that this portrait is destined to require your opinion in order to be fully completed...

t_ch: 为了纪念母亲，我与筑城者一致认为应当为她创作画像，将其送入博物馆，供贝洛伯格人民纪念。

t_ch: 尽管我们已将可可利亚大人定性为在战争中牺牲，但我们仍想进一步听取你——拯救了贝洛伯格的英雄——的意见。
t_en: Although we have already put out the narrative that Madam Cocolia has sacrificed herself in the war, we still want to hear your opinion — the Hero of Belobog who saved the city.

t_ch: 交给我吧。

t_ch: …很高兴你能这样回答。

t_ch: 我才疏学浅…

t_ch: …你不必谦虚，我相信你的直觉。至于如何组织得体的语言，交给我就好。

t_ch: 那么，我们开始吧。首先是母亲大人执政初期以前的故事——
t_en: So let's start. First is the story of mother before her reign...

t_ch: 据说，母亲大人与我一样，也是从贫瘠的地底被选拔来到上层区的。那时的她对一切充满好奇——尤其是在地底时见不到的鲜花。闲暇时，她总爱去花店。
t_en: It is said that my mother, like me, was also selected from the barren underground and brought to the Overworld. At that time, she was curious about everything, especially the flowers that she couldn't see in the Underworld. In her spare time, she would always go to the florist.

t_ch: 她后来成为了贝洛伯格士官学院首屈一指的尖子生，以年级第一的身份从那里毕业。人们都说她是贝洛伯格最智慧的头脑。
t_en: Later, she became the top student at the Belobog Military Academy, graduating at the top of her class. People say she had the most intelligent mind in all of Belobog.

t_ch: 在那之后，母亲大人踏上了执政生涯，并以她的人格魅力获得了全城人民的支持。在她督办下，银鬃铁卫终于得以反攻裂界…尽管那代价过于惨重。
t_en: After that, mother embarked on her career in governance, and with her charismatic personality, gained the support of the entire city. Under her supervision, the Silvermane Guards were finally able to counterattack and reclaim the Fragmentum... though it comes at too high a cost.

t_ch: 你觉得…在介绍中该如何描述母亲大人的早年比较好呢？
t_en: What do you think... How should we describe mother's early years in the introduction?

t_ch: 这样的话……

t_ch: 「大守护者可可利亚在遥远的下层区度过了童年，那时的她就已展现出对世间万物的好奇与热爱——初雪八落是她最钟爱的花朵，一如大守护者高洁的人格。」
t_en: "Supreme Guardian Cocolia spent her childhood in the distant Underworld, where she showed curiosity and love for all things - her favorite flower being First Snow, much like her own noble character."

t_ch: 据说，母亲大人与我一样，也是从贫瘠的地底被选拔来到上层区的。那时的她对一切充满好奇——尤其是在地底时见不到的鲜花。闲暇时，她总爱去花店。
t_en: It is said that my mother, like me, was also selected from the barren underground and brought to the Overworld. At that time, she was curious about everything, especially the flowers that she couldn't see in the Underworld. In her spare time, she would always go to the florist.

t_ch: 她后来成为了贝洛伯格士官学院首屈一指的尖子生，以年级第一的身份从那里毕业。人们都说她是贝洛伯格最智慧的头脑，就连希露瓦也难以望其项背。
t_en: Later, she became the top student at the Belobog Military Academy, graduating at the top of her class. People say she had the most intelligent mind in all of Belobog, one that not even Serval can surpass.

t_ch: 在那之后，母亲大人踏上了执政生涯，并以她的人格魅力和政治手腕获得了全城人民支持。在她督办下，银鬃铁卫终于得以反攻裂界…尽管那代价过于惨重。
t_en: After that, mother embarked on her career in governance, and with her charismatic personality and political measures, gained the support of the entire city. Under her supervision, the Silvermane Guards were finally able to counterattack and reclaim the Fragmentum... though it comes at too high a cost.

t_ch: 你觉得…在介绍中该如何描述母亲大人的早年比较好呢？
t_en: What do you think... How should we describe mother's early years in the introduction?

t_ch: 在母亲大人治下，最为知名的法令便是「封锁上下层区」。一方面，这一举措稳固了贝洛伯格的后方，可也确实造成了太多无谓的牺牲——人们对此意见多有分歧。
t_en: Under mother's rule, the most well-known decree was the "blockade of the Overworld and the Underworld." On one hand, this measure secured Belobog's rear, but it also resulted in many unnecessary sacrifices, causing much controversy among the people.

t_ch: 但她从未遗忘治愈世界的梦想。但却因某些原因在精神上持续遭受折磨，最终积劳成疾，变成了你熟知的、那无情而寡欲的模样。
t_en: However, she never forgot her dream of healing the world. But due to certain reasons, she suffered continued torment, both mentally and physically, which eventually led to her becoming the unfeeling and indifferent person you knew.

t_ch: 尽管如此，母亲大人仍将我当作最亲爱的女儿——或许她是将所剩无几的温情留给了我，而非她的子民……
t_en: Nevertheless, mother still regarded me as her dearest daughter, perhaps leaving what little warmth she had left for me, instead of her people...

t_ch: …对不起，我或许掺入了太多私人情感。
t_en: ...I'm sorry. I may have injected too many personal feelings.

t_ch: 让我们把话题扳回来吧——你觉得，在介绍中该如何描述母亲大人执政后的经历比较好呢？
t_en: Let's get back on topic. How do you think we should describe mother's experiences during her rule in the introduction?

t_ch: 重点描述「封锁政策」吧。
t_en: Emphasize the blockade policy.

t_ch: 这样的话……
t_en: In that case...

t_ch: 「在她执政中期，这位大守护者颁布了知名的『封锁令』。彼时的她坚信惟有此举能够稳固贝城后方，却也造成了太多惨烈且无谓的牺牲——无人不为之痛心。」
t_en: "In the middle of her reign, Supreme Guardian Cocolia issued the well-known order for the blockade. At that time, she firmly believed that only this action could secure Belobog's rear, but it also caused too many brutal and meaningless sacrifices — the heartbreak was not unfelt universally."

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你觉得…这样描述是否合适？
t_en: Do you think this description is appropriate, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 非常合适。
t_en: Very much so.

t_ch: 那么，接下来是母亲大人生命中的最后一刻…但这部分克里珀堡已有定论，让我们跳过它吧。
t_en: So, next is the final moments of mother's life... but Qlipoth Fort already has a conclusion for this part, so let's skip it.

t_ch: 我想再重新考虑一下。
t_en: I would like to reconsider.

t_ch: 当然可以——我们还有很多时间。
t_en: Of course. We have plenty of time.

t_ch: 重点描述「性格转变」吧。
t_en: Emphasize on her change in personality.

t_ch: 这样的话……
t_en: In that case...

t_ch: 「可她肩上的担子实在过于沉重——她试图从乱线中编织出冬城的命运，却也被它压垮。她渐渐变得无情而寡欲，但人们依旧紧密团结在她的身边。」
t_en: "But the burden on her shoulders was too heavy. She tried to weave the fate of Everwinter City from chaos, but was crushed by it. She gradually became ruthless and indifferent, but people still rallied closely around her."

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你觉得…这样描述是否合适？
t_en: Do you think this description is appropriate, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 非常合适。
t_en: Very much so.

t_ch: 那么，接下来是母亲大人生命中的最后一刻…但这部分克里珀堡已有定论，让我们跳过它吧。

t_ch: 我想再重新考虑一下。

t_en: Of course. We have plenty of time.

t_en: Emphasize her motherly love.

t_ch: 可…这只是我个人的私心……
t_en: But... don't you think that might be a bit too personal?

t_ch: …不，我相信你的判断——让我来组织一下语言吧。
t_en: ...No, I trust your judgment. Let me try and organize the language.

t_ch: 「尽管大守护者在残酷的命运面前落了下风，人性中的光辉亦被逐渐蚕食；但她仍尽力将温情留给人民，助他们求得拯救——如同她对待女儿那般。」
t_en: "Though the Supreme Guardian lost before her cruel destiny and the light of humanity eroded away, she still tried her best to give whatever gentleness she had to the people and help them toward salvation, just like how she treated her daughter."

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你觉得…这样描述是否合适？
t_en: Do you think this description is appropriate, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 非常合适。
t_en: Very much so.

t_ch: 那么，接下来是母亲大人生命中的最后一刻…但这部分克里珀堡已有定论，让我们跳过它吧。
t_en: So, next is the final moments of mother's life... but Qlipoth Fort already has a conclusion for this part, so let's skip it.

t_en: I would like to reconsider.

t_en: Of course. We have plenty of time.

t_ch: 想请你复述一遍她的故事…
t_en: I would like you to repeat her story...

t_ch: 没问题，那我就重新讲述一遍吧。
t_en: No problem. I will repeat it to you.

t_ch: 在母亲大人治下，最为知名的法令便是「封锁上下层区」。一方面，这一举措稳固了贝洛伯格的后方，但也确实造成了太多无谓的牺牲——人们对此多有分化。
t_en: Under mother's rule, the most well-known decree was the "blockade of the Overworld and the Underworld." On one hand, this measure secured Belobog's rear, but it also resulted in many unnecessary sacrifices, causing a lot of controversy among the people.

t_ch: 母亲大人从未遗忘治愈世界的梦想，但却因某些原因在精神层面上持续遭受折磨，最终积劳成疾，变成了彼时无情而寡欲的模样。
t_en: Mother never forgot her dream of healing the world. But due to certain reasons, she suffered continued torment, both mentally and physically, which eventually led to her becoming the unfeeling and indifferent person you know today.

t_ch: 尽管如此，母亲大人仍将我当作最亲爱的女儿——或许她是将所剩无几的温情留给了我，而非她的子民……
t_en: Nevertheless, mother still regarded me as her dearest daughter, perhaps leaving what little warmth she had left for me, instead of her people...

t_ch: …对不起，我或许掺入了太多私人情感。
t_en: ...I'm sorry. I may have injected too many personal feelings.

t_ch: 让我们把话题扳回来吧——你觉得，在介绍中该如何描述母亲大人执政后的经历比较好呢？
t_en: Let's get back on topic. How do you think we should describe mother's experiences during her rule in the introduction?

t_ch: 最后…让我们给母亲大人一个中肯的评价吧。尽管你们一度以刀剑相向，但我相信你能给出完美的答案。
t_en: Finally... let's give mother an objective assessment. Even though you once fought against her, I believe you can provide a perfect answer.

t_ch: 作为开拓者和她曾经的敌人，你会如何评价母亲——上一任大守护者可可利亚——呢？
t_en: As a Trailblazer and her former enemy, how would you evaluate mother — former Supreme Guardian Cocolia?

t_ch: 她有好的初衷，只是走错了路。
t_en: She had good intentions, but she chose the wrong path.

t_ch: …我很高兴你给出这样的评价。
t_en: ...I'm glad you gave this evaluation.

t_ch: 不过，你还可以再考虑一下——我们还有时间。
t_en: However, you can still think about it a little more — we have time.

t_ch: 我确定。
t_en: I'm sure about it.

t_ch: 我想再重新考虑一下。
t_en: I would like to reconsider.

t_ch: 嗯，拜托你了。
t_en: Sure, take as much time as you want.

t_ch: 我将如实陈述：她功过参半。
t_en: I will state the truth: She had both merits and faults.

t_ch: …这确实是个客观的评价。
t_en: ...This is indeed an objective evaluation.

t_ch: 不过，你还可以再考虑一下——我们还有时间。
t_en: However, you can still think about it a little more — we have time.

t_ch: 我确定。
t_en: I'm sure about it.

t_ch: 我想再重新考虑一下。
t_en: I would like to reconsider.

t_ch: 嗯，拜托你了。
t_en: Sure, take as much time as you want.

t_ch: 她是位漠视生命的冷酷女王。
t_en: She's a heartless queen with a disregard for life.

t_ch: …站在那些逝去之人的立场上，我无法反驳这样的观点。
t_en: ...From the standpoint of those who have passed away, I cannot refute such a viewpoint.

t_ch: 不过，你还可以再考虑一下——我们还有时间。
t_en: However, you can still think about it a little more — we have time.

t_ch: 我确定。
t_en: I'm sure about it.

t_ch: 我想再重新考虑一下。
t_en: I would like to reconsider.

t_ch: 嗯，拜托你了。
t_en: Sure, take as much time as you want.

t_ch: 我希望你能自己得出答案。
t_en: I hope you find your own answer.

t_ch: …真的吗？但我怕自己有失偏颇。
t_en: ...Really? I worry that I might be biased.

t_ch: 我相信你。
t_en: I believe in you.

t_ch: 我想再重新考虑一下。
t_en: I would like to reconsider.

t_ch: 嗯，拜托你了。
t_en: Sure, take as much time as you want.

t_ch: 谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。我与其他筑城者将仔细考虑你的意见，最终定稿。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}. The other Architects and I will carefully consider your opinion before finalizing the draft.

t_ch: 如果你对成稿结果有兴趣，不妨之后亲自去历史文化展馆看看吧。
t_en: If you're interested in the final text, come to the History-Culture Hall yourself to check it out.

t_ch: 那么，回见了，{NICKNAME}，感谢你为贝洛伯格作出的贡献——你永远是我们心目中的英雄。
t_en: See you later, {NICKNAME}. I thank you for your contribution to Belobog — you will forever be our hero.

t_ch: （这真的是我认识的那位希莉儿吗？去问问<color=#dbc291ff>于尔丹</color>是怎么回事吧……）
t_en: (Is this really the Cyrille I know? Let me ask <color=#dbc291ff>Uldan</color> what this is all about...)

t_ch: 有关「愚者」希莉儿的画像…
t_en: About the portrait of Cyrille the Fool...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}…你说，<color=#dbc291ff>「愚者希莉儿」</color>画像的描述与事实不符？
t_en: {NICKNAME}... Did you say that the description of <color=#dbc291ff>Cyrille the Fool's</color> portrait deviated from the truth?

t_ch: 但…这怎么可能？这可都是贝洛伯格学院历史书里的内容，白纸黑字，明明白白。
t_en: But... how's that possible? The description is taken from the history textbooks of the Belobog Academy. Everything is in black and white.

t_ch: 我甚至见过她。
t_en: I met Cyrille before.

t_ch: 这…你就别拿我这老人家打趣了。
t_en: Well... Stop pulling my leg.

t_ch: 不过，听你这口气，想必至少是听说过这号人的……
t_en: Still, I can tell you have at least heard of Cyrille from your tone.

t_ch: 我有支持希莉儿的证据。
t_en: I have evidence that supports Cyrille.

t_ch: 既然你这样言之凿凿…那就给我看看吧——如果你真有证据的话。
t_en: Since you sound so sure of yourself, I'll take a look at your evidence.

t_ch: …没想到，你竟然真的找到了如此宝贵的史料。我稍后马上安排人根据你的发现对展签内容进行修正…当然，还要把这个发现上报给克里珀堡。
t_en: ...I can't believe you have found such a precious historical source. I'll get someone to revise the content of the exhibit label based on your discovery later... Of course, I must also report this to Qlipoth Fort right away.

t_ch: 谢谢你，{NICKNAME}…如果没有你，贝洛伯格历史永远都无法给这个女孩以正确的评价了。
t_en: Thanks, {NICKNAME}... If it weren't for you, this girl wouldn't have her rightful place in the history of Belobog.

t_ch: 你确实是贝洛伯格的英雄，{NICKNAME}——当之无愧。
t_en: You're indeed the Hero of Belobog, {NICKNAME}. You deserve that title.

t_ch: 这里似乎摆放着博物馆志愿导览员的简历。如果你想，你也可以翻阅看看。
t_en: The resumes of the museum volunteer guides seem to be placed here. You may peruse them if you want.

t_ch: 翻阅。
t_en: Flip through.

t_ch: 看看希露瓦的简历。
t_en: Check out Serval's resume.

t_ch: 看看娜塔莎的简历。
t_en: Check out Natasha's resume.

t_ch: 看看虎克的简历。
t_en: Check out Hook's resume.

t_ch: 看看其他人的…
t_en: Check out the resumes of others...

t_ch: 看看杰帕德的简历。
t_en: Check out Gepard's resume.

t_ch: 看看希儿的简历。
t_en: Check out Seele's resume.

t_ch: 看看其他人的…
t_en: Check out the resumes of others...

t_ch: 就到这里吧。
t_en: That's it for now.

t_ch: 就到这里吧。
t_en: That's it for now.

t_ch: 算了吧。
t_en: Forget it.

t_ch: 如你所见，我们贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆的振兴计划到目前为止都十分顺利…在你的帮助下，综合展馆的主要展区已经基本布置完成，可以面向观众开放了。
t_en: As you can see, the revival plan of the Belobog History and Culture Museum has been going well so far... With your help, the setup of the General Hall's main exhibition area is close to completion and can soon be opened to the public.

t_ch: 哇，好漂亮啊……
t_en: Wow, this is amazing...

t_ch: 这还是我第一次来贝洛伯格的博物馆……
t_en: This has been my first time visiting the museum of Belobog...

t_ch: 这都是你的功劳，我身为这座博物馆的馆长，必须要亲自感谢你才行。
t_en: This is the fruit of your effort. As the curator of the museum, I must express my gratitude to you personally.

t_ch: 收下吧，这是为你准备的报酬——你应得的。
t_en: Here is the reward you deserve.

t_ch: 在你的帮助下，综合展馆的展品已经全部集齐——现在，贝洛伯格的观众们可以更好地了解这座城市的前世今生了。
t_en: Thanks to your help, the General Hall is filled with exhibits. Now, visitors from all over Belobog will be able to understand the background of this city better.

t_ch: 这不会是真家伙吧？
t_en: Is this thing real?

t_ch: 太厉害了……
t_en: This is awesome...

t_ch: 辛苦你了，{NICKNAME}。这是为你准备的报酬，还请你能够收下。
t_en: Thanks for your hard work, {NICKNAME}. Here is the reward for you.

t_ch: 工业展馆的展品也已经全部集齐。现在，这座主题展厅终于可以对外开放了…我们博物馆能走到这一步，你功不可没。
t_en: The Industrial Hall is filled with exhibits and ready to be opened to the public. It's all because of you that our museum manages to go this far.

t_ch: 没想到下层区是这样的风貌……
t_en: I had no idea the Underworld would look like this...

t_ch: 这些自动机兵是不是真的能动起来？
t_en: Can these automatons move?

t_ch: 为此，我和佩拉小姐又为你准备了些微薄礼，聊表谢意——辛苦你了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: To thank you for your hard work, Miss Pela and I have prepared a small gift for you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我们的大功臣——你知道吗？在你的帮助下，历史文化展馆的布置也顺利完成——这意味着我们的博物馆可以完全对外开放了！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you're our big hero. Do you know the History-Culture Hall has also been successfully set up with your help? This means that we can open the whole museum to the public!

t_ch: 没想到大守护者还有这样的故事……
t_en: I had no idea the Supreme Guardian had gone through this...

t_ch: 这就是七百年前入侵我们的怪物吗？
t_en: Is this the monster that invaded us <unbreak>700</unbreak> years ago?

t_ch: 没想到这些尘封已久的历史还能够重见天日，这座博物馆竟然还有重新焕发生机的一天…真是没想到啊……
t_en: I didn't expect this long-buried history would one day see the light of day, and the museum be brought back to life... This is all so unbelievable...

t_ch: 多亏了你啊，{NICKNAME}——你是我们贝洛伯格真正的英雄，当之无愧。
t_en: We owe this to you, {NICKNAME}. You are the true Hero of Belobog. You deserve that title.

t_ch: 一幅摄影作品，记录了贝洛伯格下层区街景一隅。
t_en: A work of photography that captures a corner of the Belobogian Underworld.

t_ch: 作品中的下层区似乎与你印象里的磐岩镇有所不同。难道地下还有白昼和黑夜的区别吗？
t_en: The Underworld in the piece seems to diverge from your memory of Boulder Town. Does the day-and-night cycle also exist in the underground?

t_ch: 一幅摄影作品，记录了磐岩镇搏击俱乐部的内景。
t_en: A work of photography that captures the interior of Boulder Town's Fight Club.

t_ch: 空无一人的搏击俱乐部似乎也别有一番风味。但那里真会有没人的时候吗？
t_en: When empty, Fight Club has a different ambiance to it. But has it ever been empty?

t_ch: 一幅摄影作品，记录了机械屋「永动」的样子。
t_en: A work of photography that captures the "neverwinter" warmth of the workshop.

t_ch: 你已经见过这场景太多次了——回到机械屋对你而言就像回裂界一样轻松。
t_en: You've already seen it so many times. To you, returning to the workshop is as simple as going to the Fragmentum.

t_ch: 一幅摄影作品，记录了废弃矿镇现时的风貌。
t_en: A work of photography that captures the present-day appearance of the abandoned mining town.

t_ch: 说起来，废弃的矿镇街道上为何会亮着灯呢？
t_en: Speaking of, why are the street lamps lit in an abandoned mining town?

t_ch: 一幅摄影作品，记录了铆钉镇上那座工坊的景色。
t_en: A work of photography that captures the view of the workshop in Rivet Town.

t_ch: 你似乎没有在这幅作品中看到那位机械朋友的身影。
t_en: You don't see your robot friend's form in this piece of work.

t_ch: 一幅摄影作品，记录了沦为裂界的边堡城塞。
t_en: A work of photography that captures the fallen citadel that has become part of the Fragmentum.

t_ch: 这幅作品的气质很是清冷——和那片死寂的怪异空间如出一辙。
t_en: This piece of work is chilling — exactly what the dreary, strange space evokes.

t_ch: 一幅摄影作品，记录了筑城者建立的古老遗迹一角。
t_en: A work of photography that captures a corner of the ancient ruins built by the Architects.

t_ch: 「造物引擎」与虚卒的冰雕。作为影像中唯二的入镜者，永冻的它们或许能得到些慰藉。
t_en: The Engine of Creation and an ice sculpture of a Voidranger. Eternally frozen, they may have been consoled by the fact that they were the only two entities photographed.

t_ch: 一幅摄影作品，记录了上城区的花店「长夏」的店面。
t_en: A work of photography that captures the shop front of Eversummer Florist on the Overworld.

t_ch: 这张照片给了你一种错觉：这个房间中仿佛真的藏着永冬之城的夏天。
t_en: This picture gives you an illusion that this room really did house a summertime in the Everwinter City.

t_ch: 一幅摄影作品，记录了歌德宾馆金碧辉煌的大堂。
t_en: A work of photography that captures the grandiose hall of Goethe Hotel.

t_ch: 华丽、温馨、舒适，这是很多人对这幅作品的第一印象——他们肯定没有入住次日就被全城通缉的经历。
t_en: "Opulent, warm, and cozy" is the first impression people have of this piece. They must not have experienced being wanted all over the city the second day of staying there.

t_ch: 一幅摄影作品，记录了磐岩镇搏击俱乐部门口的景象。
t_en: A work of photography that captures the entrance view of Boulder Town's Fight Club.

t_ch: 不知道要付给那个铁公鸡多少冬城盾，才能疏散观众拍出门可罗雀的搏击俱乐部。
t_en: You wonder how many shield one has to pay to the bouncer to evacuate the audience and photograph the empty Fight Club.

t_ch: 一幅摄影作品，记录了克里珀堡中大守护者的办公室。
t_en: A work of photography that captures the Supreme Guardian's office in Qlipoth Fort.

t_ch: 照片中不见那位少女匆忙的身影。她现在或许正在此处，努力将这座城市带往更美好的未来。
t_en: You don't see the busy figure of the lady in the picture. She must be toiling herself in this place right now to lead the city into a better future.

t_ch: {TEXTJOIN#40}，出自巡回艺术画派巨擘雷比尼之手，描绘了黄金歌剧院在蓝天下熠熠生辉的模样。
t_en: {TEXTJOIN#40} by Rebini, a paragon of the nomadism movement, enlivened the shimmering Golden Theater with the blue sky as the background.

t_ch: 你印象中的歌剧院穹顶似乎并不像画中一样饱满。这大概就是所谓的「艺术加工」吧。
t_en: You don't recall the Golden Theater's dome being as full as it was portrayed in the picture. Perhaps this is what they call "artistic license."

t_ch: 一幅油画作品，描绘了宏阔的行政区全景。
t_en: An oil painting depicting the Administrative District in its full majesty.

t_ch: 画中的行政区比你印象里的要大很多，或许是因为这里的很多地方你都去不了。
t_en: The Administrative District depicted in the painting is much larger than you remember. Maybe it's because there are many places off-limits to you.

t_ch: 一幅油画作品，描绘了磐岩镇的街头景色。
t_en: An oil painting depicting the streets of Boulder Town.

t_ch: 昏暗的街道上空无一人，令这幅作品缺少了下层区聚落特有的亲切感。
t_en: The dim, empty street on the piece lacks the unique hospitality of the Underworld settlements.

t_ch: 《劳动者》，出自巡回艺术画派巨擘雷比尼之手，描绘了大矿区的景象。
t_en: The Laborers by Rebini, a paragon of the nomadism movement, depicts the scenery of the Great Mine.

t_ch: 看了这幅画，上层区的人们可能会对尘土飞扬的矿坑产生不切实际的美好幻想。
t_en: Seeing this picture might cause the Overworlders to harbor an impractical, romanticized notion of the dusty mines.

t_ch: 一幅油画作品，描绘了查拉图斯特拉萨帕……
t_en: An oil painting depicting Mt. Zarathustrazapa...

t_ch: …查拉图斯特拉萨帕拉伊斯坎德拉山的远景。据说是雅利洛-Ⅵ表面最高的山峰，但这名字到底是谁起的？
t_en: ...Mt. Zarathustrazapala Iskandra from afar. It was said to be the tallest mountain on the surface of Jarilo-VI, but who gave it such a ridiculous name?

t_ch: 《晨曦》，出自巡回艺术画派巨擘雷比尼之手，描绘了沐浴在晨曦中的贝洛伯格。
t_en: Ray of Dawn by Rebini, a paragon of the nomadism movement, depicts the Belobog basking in the morning sun.

t_ch: 你只能从雪幕中看到雪，而那位画家却看到了希望的曙光——这大概就是后者能够成为艺术家的原因了。
t_en: You can only see snow from the snow curtain, but the artist saw a ray of hope beyond it — this must be why they managed to become an artist.

t_ch: 一块地髓结晶原石，品相很是高级。
t_en: A piece of geomarrow ore, looks to be very high in quality.

t_ch: 这种矿物是驱动整个贝洛伯格不断运转的燃料，更是万千劳动者汗水的结晶——其中当然也包括你。
t_en: This mineral is the source that fuels the entire Belobog's operation, as well as the crystallization of blood, sweat, and tears of thousands of workers — including you.

t_ch: 一辆贝洛伯格最常见的公共交通工具（的比例模型），经常能见到它带着「叮叮当当」的声音驶过街头。
t_en: A scale model of the most common form of public transport in Belobog. They can be seen all over the streets making ding-dong sounds as they pass by.

t_ch: 如今的冬城人依旧钟情这种传统且可靠的交通方式——尽管在通勤高峰时，它的可靠性会有所动摇。
t_en: Belobogians still love this traditional and trustworthy form of transportation, even though its trustworthiness can waver during rush hours.

t_ch: 一尊黄铜制的天球仪，造型典雅。
t_en: An armillary sphere made with brass. Classy and elegant.

t_ch: 你似乎在大守护者的办公室里见过同样的摆件。可到底哪个才是复制品？
t_en: You seem to recall seeing an identical ornament in the Supreme Guardian's office, but which one is the replica?

t_ch: 一尊黄铜制的天球仪，造型典雅。
t_en: An armillary sphere made with brass. Classy and elegant.

t_ch: 你已经在大守护者的办公室里仔细端详过这件展品了，很清楚星球表面那可爱的图案到底意味着什么。
t_en: You've already inspected this display in detail in the Supreme Guardian's office. You know exactly what the lovely diagram on the surface of the planet really means.

t_ch: 结构复杂的机械构件。互相啮合的齿轮传递着力量，维系着这座城市的运作。
t_en: A complicated machinery component. The intermeshing system of gears transmits power to sustain the city's functions.

t_ch: 再庞大的机器也是由微小的部件驱动的，就像贝洛伯格与她的人民。
t_en: Even a huge machine is driven by the tiny components with which it is made, just like Belobog and her citizens.

t_ch: 笨重粗犷的操作杆台座。它能用来驱动许多机械结构，而你也确实如是做了——主要是那些升降台和矿车轨道。
t_en: A bulky and rugged operating lever base. It can be used to drive many mechanical constructs, and many of which you used — mainly of elevators and mine cart tracks.

t_ch: 你怀疑贝洛伯格的工程师们是不是已经彻底放弃思考了。
t_en: You wonder if the Belobogian engineers have given up on thinking.

t_ch: 安装着许多接口的机械铁球，学名是「机关能源中枢」，能够将地髓矿脉转化为各种设备所需的能源。
t_en: A mechanical iron ball with many connective ports, formally called a mechanism energy hub. It can convert geomarrow veins into energy required for all kinds of devices.

t_ch: 你有时会感到好奇——为什么如此重要的机器内部竟然充满了连你这种门外汉都能修好的电路？
t_en: Your curiosity is piqued — Why is the inside of such an important machine filled with circuits that even a layperson like you can fix?

t_ch: 银鬃铁卫的加农火炮。这种武器或许有些落后，但其威力足以捍卫贝洛伯格的安宁。
t_en: Silvermane Guards' cannon. This weapon might be a little outdated, but its firepower is enough to defend the peace in Belobog.

t_ch: 你或许并未意识到，在不算漫长的逃亡之旅中，它的炮口常常对着你。
t_en: You may not have realized it, but in your short journey as a wanted person, its muzzle is frequently pointed at you.

t_ch: 一台自动机兵的操作终端机柜，与机兵们复杂的系统直接相连。
t_en: An operational terminal cabinet of an automaton, it is directly connected to the complex systems of the automated soldiers.

t_ch: 自动机兵真的很自动，所以没有多少人会专门操作和维护这个东西。
t_en: Automatons are all automated, so not many people know how to operate and maintain this thing.

t_ch: 一台古董收音机，七百年前对抗反物质军团时幸存的遗物之一。
t_en: An antique radio, one of the relics that survived the Antimatter Legion's invasion <unbreak>700</unbreak> years ago.

t_ch: 在那段难见天日的黑暗岁月里，正是这台小小的机器将整个城市的每一颗心维系在了一起。
t_en: In those dark times, it was this tiny machine that tied each and every heart of the city together.

t_ch: 一个破旧的皮箱，是来自四百年前的文物，出土时还装着其他的文物。
t_en: A worn leather case from four hundred years ago. It contained other artifacts when it was unearthed.

t_ch: 你在这里没有看到皮箱里的东西。大概是策展人为了显得展品够多，把它们放在了其他展柜里了。
t_en: You don't see anything inside the leather case. The curator probably placed it in other display cases to bolster their number of collections.

t_ch: 七百年前对抗反物质军团时使用的行军毯，是过去铁卫们最可靠的床榻。
t_en: The military bedroll used <unbreak>700</unbreak> years ago against the Antimatter Legion. It was the most reliable bed for the Silvermane Guards in the past.

t_ch: 典型的前寒潮时期产物，毕竟现在的天气下已经不存在如此单薄的床榻了。
t_en: A typical pre-Eternal Freeze production. After all, with the current weather, such thin bedrolls no longer serve a purpose.

t_ch: 旧世界的古董油灯。在银河中的知识降临雅利洛-Ⅵ之前，它是最寻常的照明工具。
t_en: An antique oil lamp from the Old World. Before interplanetary knowledge descended onto Jarilo-VI, it was the most common tool for lighting.

t_ch: 过去的人们曾借它在黑夜中寻求光明。而后，天外之物驱逐了这里的长夜，却也使他们失去了许多。
t_en: In the past, people depended on them to shed light in the darkness. Later on, technology of an extraterrestrial nature helped to chase away the long nights, but it also cost them a lot.

t_ch: 曾经是平平无奇的工艺品，如今是博物馆的重要馆藏。
t_en: It used to be a mundane handicraft project, but is now one of the more important pieces in the museum.

t_ch: 或许它在那个年代只是一件垃圾。但只要存在得够久，垃圾也能成为宝物。
t_en: Maybe in those years, it was only a piece of junk. But with time, even junk can become treasure.

t_ch: 一款老式台灯，地髓能源应用于日常生活的重要标志。
t_en: A primitive table lamp. It is an important symbol of application of geomarrow energy to everyday life.

t_ch: 想必你已经发现了：在贝洛伯格，从能源中枢到家用电器，它们用的电路系统都是一样的。
t_en: You must have noticed: In Belobog, everything from energy hubs to household electrical appliances uses the same circuit system.

t_ch: 诞生自筑城纪元<unbreak>280</unbreak>年间的首饰盒，岁月无法掩盖它的精致。
t_en: A jewelry box made in <unbreak>280</unbreak> AF. Its delicate nature cannot be concealed by its age.

t_ch: 它曾被用来承载美丽的饰物，如今则被用来承载逝去的时间。
t_en: It used to be a receptacle of beautiful accessories, but is now only a receptacle of lost time.

t_ch: 来自天外的陨石碎片，七百年前随反物质军团一同降临在这片大地上。
t_en: Meteor shards from outer space. It landed on this land alongside the Antimatter Legion <unbreak>700</unbreak> years ago.

t_ch: 寒潮驱逐了入侵者，但泯灭不了它们留下的痕迹，这颗星星的碎片便是其中之一。
t_en: The Eternal Freeze chased the intruders away, but could not wipe away the traces they left behind. This piece of star shard is one of many left behind.

t_ch: 一枚古董标本瓶，瓶中花束意外地生机盎然。
t_en: An antique herbarium bottle with an unexpectedly vibrant bouquet.

t_ch: 你猜花束标本是新放进去的，真正的古董只有这个瓶子。
t_en: You surmise the bouquet was newly placed inside. The only antique is the bottle.

t_ch: 一幅摄影作品，记录了科考基地「炉心」的景色。
t_en: A work of photography that captures the scenery of the Furnace Core research station.

t_ch: 当年的科考基地已成为史瓦罗与女孩的家——但他们为什么总站在门口不进去呢？
t_en: The research station of yesteryear has become Svarog and the girl's home, but why are they always outside and never go inside?

t_ch: 一幅摄影作品，记录了贝洛伯格广场上的纪念碑「永冬铭碑」。
t_en: A work of photography that captures the Everwinter Monument erected on the Belobog Plaza.

t_ch: 某位情报官曾介绍过纪念碑的设计理念。忘记了也没关系，就算没人介绍，优秀的艺术品也能自行传递其蕴含的价值。
t_en: A certain intelligence officer has introduced the monument's design concept. It doesn't matter if you forget, because even if no one introduces it, a good piece of art can convey the value it contains by its own virtue.

t_ch: 一幅摄影作品，记录了永冬岭上的星核拘束装置。
t_en: A work of photography that captures the Stellaron Imprisonment Device atop Everwinter Hill.

t_ch: 它拘束了从天而降的灾祸、贝洛伯格的未来，以及一位大守护者的漫长的噩梦。
t_en: Imprisoned inside it is a calamity descended from the sky, the future of Belobog, and the enduring nightmare of a Supreme Guardian.

t_ch: 一幅摄影作品，记录了贝洛伯格的客运缆车。
t_en: A work of photography that captures a Belobogian cable car.

t_ch: 尘封十余年的缆车在你们的努力下重新启动。它于上下层区间不倦地摆渡，老旧的缆绳重新联结起长别的人们。
t_en: With your effort, the cable car restarted its service after a decade of collecting dust. It travels between the Overworld and Underworld ceaselessly, and its rundown cable reconnecting the long-segregated people of Belobog.

t_ch: 一幅摄影作品，记录了贝洛伯格边境的铁卫禁区。
t_en: A work of photography that captures the Silvermane Guard Restricted Zone on the edge of Belobog.

t_ch: 市民们或许不知道，在这里遍布错综复杂的道路与机关障碍：难道银鬃铁卫们都不会迷路吗？
t_en: The citizenry may not have known, but intricate road networks and mechanical obstacles are placed all over the premises: Do Silvermane Guards not get lost?

t_ch: 一幅摄影作品，记录了大矿区的大桥。
t_en: A work of photography that captures the bridge at the Great Mine.

t_ch: 冰冷的钢铁桥梁与山崖显得有些凄清，但在矿工眼中十分亲切——它是工人们回家的必经之路。
t_en: The cold, hard steel bridge and the cliff look a little bleak, but the miners hold them in great affection — it is the path leading home to the miners.

t_ch: 一幅摄影作品，记录了大矿区的地髓矿脉。
t_en: A work of photography that captures the geomarrow veins in the Great Mine.

t_ch: 巨大的矿脉俨然岩层下的另一轮太阳——它照亮了地下矿区、上下层的街道，以及整座贝洛伯格。
t_en: The massive mineral vein is like an underground sun buried in stone — it illuminated the mines, the streets in both the Overworld and the Underworld, and the entirety of Belobog.

t_ch: 一幅摄影作品，记录了铁卫禁区的能源中枢。
t_en: A work of photography that captures the energy hub of the Silvermane Guard Restricted Zone.

t_ch: 你还记得自己为了靠近这个大铁球费了不少力气，好在那位倒霉蛋总是随身携带着那枚重要的钥匙。
t_en: You remember using much effort to get close to the giant steel ball. Fortunately, that poor fool always carries the all-important key.

t_ch: 一张概念图稿，记录了艺术雕塑「齿轮与智慧」的迭代历程。
t_en: A piece of concept art. It records the iterative process of the sculpture, Gears and Wisdom.

t_ch: 这幅设计稿能教给你的生活小窍门：通常情况下，你只能像这组齿轮一样拼命消磨自己的智慧，直到确认需求方的真实意图。
t_en: Here's a life protip this concept art can teach you: Often, you'll just have to scramble and grind your wits like this set of gears until you manage to ascertain the true intent of the requester.

t_ch: 「约翰•匹克之选」牌缝纫机的广告画，但是复制品。
t_en: An advertisement illustration of a John Peak's Choice brand sewing machine, but this is a replica.

t_ch: 「约翰•匹克之选」牌缝纫机是贝洛伯格工业转向轻型化的重要标志，这也是它为何会被张贴在这里的原因。
t_en: John Peak's Choice brand sewing machines are an important symbol of the shift toward a light industry in Belobog, which is why this is posted here.

t_ch: 一幅贝洛伯格机械制造业的宣传画，但是复制品。
t_en: A poster for Belobog's machinery manufacturing industry, but this is a replica.

t_ch: 望着铅一般的色调与画幅中的重型器械，你仿佛回到了那个由钢铁铸成的时代。
t_en: Looking at the leaden tones and the heavy machinery depicted on the canvas, you feel like you were transported back to that era when almost everything was made of steel.

t_ch: 一座以齿轮为主题的艺术雕塑，名叫「齿轮与智慧」。
t_en: A gear-themed sculpture named Gears and Wisdom.

t_ch: 可它为什么叫「齿轮与智慧」，而不是齿轮与其他的什么东西？不过，能让人产生思考，也算是与智慧有关。
t_en: But why Gears and Wisdom and not Gears and Some Other Things? Then again, being that it elicits reflection and thoughts, calling it Wisdom doesn't seem to be too far-fetched.

t_ch: 巨大的地髓供暖器，贝洛伯格街头随处可见的加热装置。
t_en: A huge geomarrow radiator. It is a heating device that can be seen everywhere on the streets of Belobog.

t_ch: 你感到暖意流经全身。要是在星球表面铺满这样的供暖器，或许真的可以驱散寒潮。
t_en: You feel warmth swirling around your torso. If the surface of the planet can be covered with heating devices like this, the Eternal Freeze might really be dispelled.

t_ch: 路边随处可见的「铜墙」型汽车，由「拉塔」汽车公司生产。
t_en: A Bronze Wall model automobile that can be seen all over the city. It is manufactured by Ratta Motors.

t_ch: 这款汽车自诞生起就以品质过硬、价格低廉著称，长年冠绝「拉塔」汽车销量榜冠军，以至于它们到现在为止只发售了这一款民用型号。
t_en: This model has been famous for its high build quality and affordable price. Ratta Motors's sales always tops the chart, so much so that they have only released this one civilian model so far.

t_ch: 一辆「灯塔」型汽车，是「拉塔」汽车公司生产的限量款。
t_en: A Beacon model automobile. It is a limited edition automobile manufactured by Ratta Motors.

t_ch: 据传，这辆车原是大守护者阿丽娜的专属藏品，但那位贵公子却弃之如敝履——看来，贝洛伯格的经济水平在七百年间有了质的飞跃。
t_en: Rumor has it that this car is part of Supreme Guardian Alina's personal collection, but a certain rich person treats it like it is worthless. Hopefully, that means Belobog's economic growth has been booming for the past <unbreak>700</unbreak> years.

t_ch: 一辆「提灯」型汽车，如今主要用于出租车业务。
t_en: A Lantern model automobile. It is mostly used as a rented ride vehicle.

t_ch: 然而，贝洛伯格的绝大多数居民都选择乘坐有轨电车通勤。出租车高昂的价格令许多人望而却步。
t_en: However, a vast majority of Belobog citizens choose to commute by tram. Taxicabs' high price would stop most people in their tracks.

t_ch: 一台自动机兵，型号名为「蜘蛛」。
t_en: An automaton of the model Spider.

t_ch: 就你的经验，这个型号的机兵似乎并不安全。希望它不会在这里爆炸。
t_en: In your experience, this automaton model is not safe. Hopefully, it doesn't explode in the establishment.

t_ch: 一台自动机兵，型号名为「甲虫」。
t_en: An automaton of the model Beetle.

t_ch: 据说「甲虫」的信号灯曾被用于协助调度采集建造作业——但如今的它们似乎总是同时亮着两盏灯。
t_en: It is said that Beetles' signal lights were once used to assist in scheduling collection and construction operations — Nowadays, both the lights seem to be on constantly.

t_ch: 一台自动机兵，型号名为「战犬」。
t_en: An automaton of the model Hound.

t_ch: 机械宠物的圈子里最受欢迎的型号——可能只是因为名字里有个「犬」。看来，名字对一个产品来说真的很重要。
t_en: In the mech-pet circle, this is the most popular model, just because it's modeled after a puppy. Looks like appearance is really important to merchandise.

t_ch: 一组老式金属机柜，是矿用机械的终端设备。
t_en: A set of old metal cabinets. These are terminal equipment for mining machinery.

t_ch: 在贝洛伯格，简单的矿用机械通常都配有复杂的终端设备。但它们的存在并不能简化作业流程，只是为机械师们提供了炫技的空间。
t_en: In Belobog, simple mining machinery usually comes with complicated terminal equipment. However, their existence is not there to simplify operations, but to provide a space for mechanics to show off their skills.

t_ch: 一台下层区生产的古典座钟，人们利用它在昏暗的地下数算时间。
t_en: A classic desk clock made in the Underworld. People use it to calculate time in the lightless underground.

t_ch: 这座钟对于矿工而言意义非凡——毕竟地髓什么时候都可以挖，错过的休息时间可找不回来。
t_en: This clock is especially meaningful to miners. After all, geomarrow will not be running out anytime soon, but precious rest time can never be replaced.

t_ch: 一面显示屏，装在笨重的机柜上。
t_en: A display screen mounted on a bulky cabinet.

t_ch: 在你的梦里，曾有一位深蓝头发的男人对着这屏幕喃喃自语——可它甚至都不会发光，天啊。
t_en: In your dreams, a man with deep-blue hair was murmuring at the screen. It couldn't even light up, dear gods.

t_ch: 一台精致的放映机，出自黄金歌剧院的慷慨借用。
t_en: An exquisite projector, on generous loan from the Golden Theater.

t_ch: 七百年来，这部放映机从未放映过任何电影——因为缺乏拷贝和拍摄电影的基础，电影本身已名存实亡。
t_en: In the past <unbreak>700</unbreak> years, this projector had never shown any films. Due to the lack of copying and filming basics, the film industry only exists in name.

t_ch: 现已停产的某型号显示屏，陈年古董。
t_en: A display screen of a certain model that has been discontinued. An antique.

t_ch: 虽说是古董，但它相较于如今的产品在技术上并不落后——冬城的消费者对于不思进取的产商们似乎非常宽容。
t_en: Although it is an antique, it is not technologically backward compared to today's products — the Everwinter City consumers seem to be very tolerant of unoriginal manufacturers.

t_ch: 一台缝纫机，款式精美。
t_en: An elaborate sewing machine.

t_ch: 对于这个寒冷的世界而言，能够带来温暖的纺织品本身就有种「存护」的意味。
t_en: For a freezing world, textiles that bring warmth have an inherent meaning of "Preservation."

t_ch: 一台缝纫机，款式简约。
t_en: A simple sewing machine.

t_ch: 在贝洛伯格，你随处都能买到贴身的衣物。但再奢侈的衣物，都不如一件别人用它专门为你编织的心意昂贵。
t_en: In Belobog, you can always buy clothing that fits you anywhere, but not even the most extravagant clothing is comparable to a piece woven with the sewing machine specifically for you.

t_ch: 一台民用放映机，比黄金歌剧院出借的款式要简陋。
t_en: A civilian projector, cruder than the one on loan from the Golden Theater.

t_ch: 长得很像电影里的道具，但它确实是用来放电影的。
t_en: Looks like a prop in a film, but it really is to show movies.

t_ch: 一系列来自下层区的招贴画，看起来已十分陈旧。
t_en: A series of posters from the Underworld. They look old.

t_ch: 它们为什么会出现在这里？你不好说。
t_en: Why would they appear here? You can't say for sure.

t_ch: 一系列来自下层区的招贴画，看起来已十分陈旧。
t_en: A series of posters from the Underworld. They look old.

t_ch: 难道这座博物馆真没有其他更合适的展品了吗？
t_en: Does this museum not have any other more suitable exhibits?

t_ch: 第一任大守护者阿丽萨•兰德的肖像画。其展签上如是写着——
t_en: A portrait of Alisa Rand, the first Supreme Guardian. The exhibition label reads:

t_ch: 「在末日谣言四起之时，大守护者阿丽萨•兰德与她忠诚的筑城者们一道号召众人高筑城墙，成就了如今对抗风雪的最后堡垒——贝洛伯格；」
t_en: "When the rumors of the doomsday spread, the Supreme Guardian Alisa Rand and her loyal Architects called upon everyone to build a great wall, which has since become the last bastion against the wind and snow — Belobog."

t_ch: 「面对天外之敌的进犯，阿丽萨高执炎枪，率领贝洛伯格众人凭城据守，直到春神与战争之神雅利洛降临至我们的行列中；」
t_en: "In the face of the invasion of the enemy from beyond the sky, Alisa held her flaming spear and led the people of Belobog to defend the city until the god of spring and war, Jarilo, descended upon our ranks."

t_ch: 「她的坚韧与英勇唤来了驱散敌寇的汹涌寒潮，彻底终结了长达三十余年的惨烈战争，和平奇迹般地自战火肆虐的大地再度滋长；」
t_en: "Her tenacity and valor summoned the turbulent Eternal Freeze to disperse the enemy invaders and completely ended the tragic war that lasted more than thirty years. Miraculously, peace sprouted once again from the land ravaged by war."

t_ch: 「在生命的最后，大守护者决意前往裂界自行了断，以向众人宣告贝洛伯格绝不会被个人意志扼住咽喉；」
t_en: "At the end of her life, the Supreme Guardian decided to go to the Fragmentum and meet her own end, in order to announce to the people that Belobog would never be choked by the throat by personal will."

t_ch: 「 第一位大守护者自此陨落。同时，贝洛伯格正式迈进风与雪与火种的时代。」
t_en: "Our first Supreme Guardian has since fallen. At the same time, Belobog officially took its first step into the age of wind, snow, and kindling."

t_ch: 「——『引发奇迹之人』阿丽萨•兰德。」
t_en: "— She Who Evokes Miracles, Alisa Rand."

t_ch: 第五任大守护者阿丽娜•兰德的肖像画。其展签上如是写着——
t_en: A portrait of Alina Rand, the fifth Supreme Guardian. The exhibition label reads:

t_ch: 「赞美仁慈的阿丽娜•兰德！她的仁慈使骁勇的银鬃铁卫举起长戟，拱卫宏伟的克里珀堡与那隔绝风雪的高墙；」
t_en: "Praise the kind Alina Rand! Her benevolence has caused the valiant Silvermane Guards to raise their halberds to defend the magnificent Qlipoth Fort and the high wall keeping storm and blizzard out."

t_ch: 「她的仁慈使贝洛伯格纷乱的法理重新归于明晰，将那作威作福的显贵者裁撤，更责令其将权柄归还给其原本的所有者；」
t_en: "Her mercy brought clarity back to the troubled jurisprudence of Belobog, abolished the dignitaries who had been abusing their powers, and enjoined them to return their authority to their rightful owners."

t_ch: 「她的仁慈使贝洛伯格停转的齿轮机构再度发出轰鸣，象征着进步的烟焰自高耸的炉身贯入雪幕；」
t_en: "Her compassion made Belobog's stalled gears roar once more, and the smoke of progress flowed from the towering furnace into the snow curtain."

t_ch: 「她的慈爱广博如深空，她的仁善闪耀如黄金——赞美仁慈的阿丽娜•兰德！」
t_en: "Her love is as broad as the sky is deep, and her kindness shines like gold — praise be to the benevolent Alina Rand!"

t_ch: 「——『慈母』阿丽娜•兰德。」
t_en: "— The Loving, Alina Rand."

t_ch: 第二任大守护者斯维特兰娜•兰德的肖像画。其展签上如是写着——
t_en: A portrait of Svetlana Rand, the second Supreme Guardian. The exhibition label reads:

t_ch: 「阿丽萨•兰德的养女，后进者的领袖，银鬃铁卫的统领者与贝洛伯格的戍卫者与重振者；」
t_en: "Alisa Rand's adopted daughter, the leader of the greener Architects, the commander of the Silvermane Guards, and Belobog's protector and rebuilder."

t_ch: 「彼时，人们只记得她与阿丽萨的联结，将其与前任大守护者多作比较，但她最终以非凡的手腕使众人折服，将离散的人心重新聚于一处；」
t_en: "During that time, people only recognized her by her connection with Alisa. They compared her to the previous Supreme Guardian, but eventually, she won over the people's hearts in her own unique way, reuniting the hearts of those that have drifted apart."

t_ch: 「她将混乱的火种亲手碾作枯焦的薪柴——她为饱受战火蹂躏的城市建立起无数遮风避日的屋宇，又将食粮与温热的地髓带给一切蒙受苦难的人；」
t_en: "With her own hands, she crushed the kindlings of chaos into charred wood — she built countless shelters for the war-torn city, and brought food and warm geomarrow to all who suffered."

t_ch: 「直到大守护者踏上母亲走过的路，再度踏入裂界时，人们终于放下成见，一致高唱她那高贵的姓名为其送行——」
t_en: "It was only after the Supreme Guardian walked in her mother's footsteps and entered the Fragmentum once more that the people finally let go of their preconceived thinking and sang her noble name."

t_ch: 「——『铁腕』斯维特兰娜•兰德。」
t_en: "— Iron Fist, Svetlana Rand."

t_ch: 第三任大守护者亚历珊德拉•兰德的肖像画。其展签上如是写着——
t_en: A portrait of Alexandra Rand, the third Supreme Guardian. The exhibition label reads:

t_ch: 「她是贝洛伯格大学的创建者与永久名誉校长，全贝洛伯格最慷慨的学术赞助人：她置纷乱的语言与度量衡于同一准绳之下，打破了隔绝众人心智的藩篱；」
t_en: "The founder of the University of Belobog and its permanent honorary dean, the most generous academic sponsor in all of Belobog: She compiled confusing languages and metrics, measured them against the same standards, and broke the barriers that separate people's minds."

t_ch: 「她是贝洛伯格地下世界的执炬人，当之无愧的开拓者：她令贝洛伯格每一颗勇敢的心都投身于地髓开拓团的伟大事业，并在那岩层之下为坚韧的冬城人赢得了广阔的天地；」
t_en: "She is the torchbearer of the Belobog Underworld, a peerless pioneer. She was the reason every valorous soul in Belobog devote themselves to the great cause of the Geomarrow Development Group, and the reason our Everwinter City found expansion on the rocky underground."

t_ch: 「她是贝洛伯格历史的记录者，贝洛伯格大事年表的修订者：她为贝洛伯格在时间的洪流中寻得自己的立足之处，从此勾勒出冬城的未来所在；」
t_en: "She is the record keeper of Belobog's history, the compiler of Belobog Chronicles. She has found her footing in Belobog among the tsunami of time and sketched the direction in which the Everwinter City was headed."

t_ch: 「她是觉悟者，求真者，真正的尊贵之人，更是唯二将自己的名号刻在冬城城墙上的守护者：行政区的心脏——亚历珊德拉广场——便以她的名字命名。」
t_en: "She is an enlightened, a truthseeker, a true exalted, and one of the only two Supreme Guardians to have her name carved onto the ever-standing walls of the Everwinter City. The heart of the Administrative District — the Alexandra Plaza — was named after her."

t_ch: 「——『智者』亚历珊德拉•兰德。」
t_en: "— The Wise, Alexandra Rand."

t_ch: 第八任大守护者希莉儿•兰德的肖像画。其展签上如是写着——
t_en: A portrait of Cyrille Rand, the eighth Supreme Guardian. The exhibition label reads:

t_ch: 「她的人生是一场庸碌浮沉的悲剧；她的统治让贝洛伯格的人民尝尽疾苦；」
t_en: "Her life was a tragedy full of mediocrity, arduity, ups, and downs. Her administration caused every anguish known to humanity to the Belobogian citizenry."

t_ch: 「幼年时的希莉儿资质平平，却因一连串阴差阳错的巧合被选中，成为了大守护者的候选人；」
t_en: "In her youth, Cyrille was unexceptional. However, due to a series of opportune events, she became a candidate to become the Supreme Guardian."

t_ch: 「她的单纯和善良被彼时身居高位的奸谄之辈利用，后者将所有权力揽于一身，不断推出压榨平民的苛政，只为满足自身的贪欲；」
t_en: "Her naivete and kindness was abused by those in power with malevolent intent, who took all the authority as their own, and who continuously carry out an oppressive rule against the citizens, just to assuage their own greed."

t_ch: 「终于，积蓄的阴火于某个静谧的雪夜炸响，在一场精心设计的骗局中，希莉儿被自己的子民夺去了生命；」
t_en: "Finally, a hidden deposition ignited and exploded one snowy night. In an elaborate, premeditated trickery, Cyrille lost her life to her own subjects."

t_ch: 「彼时的人们为『暴君』的消逝欢呼雀跃，百年后，则有更多人将她的故事写作时代的挽歌。」
t_en: "Then, the people cheered for the fall of the tyrant. A century later, many more wrote her story as an elegy of the ages."

t_ch: 「——『愚者』希莉儿•兰德。」
t_en: "— The Fool, Cyrille Rand."

t_ch: 「后来，来自星空的旅行者找到了线索——原来希莉儿的回响始终游荡在支离破碎的裂界空间中，从未消散。」
t_en: "Later, travelers from space found the clue. It turned out that the echo of Cyrille's voice had always been resounding in the broken Fragmentum space. It never dissipated."

t_ch: 「{F#她}{M#他}帮助一个迷茫且无助的灵魂找到了安息之所，给她枯寂的来生也画上了句号。」
t_en: "{F#She}{M#He} helped the lost and helpless soul to find a resting place and put an end to her lonely afterlife."

t_ch: 「倘若世上确有能供灵魂安眠的温床，愿她这次合上双眼时能与凄凉的命运彻底诀别；愿她能微笑着进入梦乡。」
t_en: "If there exists a place in the world where souls can rest peacefully, I hope she will bid farewell to her miserable destiny when she closes her eyes. May she go to sleep with a smile on her face."

t_ch: 第十八任大守护者可可利亚与其女布洛妮娅的肖像画。其展签上如是写着——
t_en: A portrait of Cocolia, the eighteenth Supreme Guardian, and her daughter Bronya. The exhibition label reads:

t_ch: {TEXTJOIN#41}
t_en: {TEXTJOIN#41}

t_ch: {TEXTJOIN#42}
t_en: {TEXTJOIN#42}

t_ch: 「在她生命的最后一刻里，这位大守护者毅然选择以其单薄的身躯对抗来自天外的、不可名状的力量。她为冬城捐躯，也终于驱散了呼啸七百年不绝的风雪。」
t_en: "In the final moments of her life, the Supreme Guardian chose to resist the undefinable power from beyond the clouds with her singular form. She sacrificed herself for the Everwinter City, and finally ended the snowstorm that howled for seven centuries."

t_ch: {TEXTJOIN#43}
t_en: {TEXTJOIN#43}

t_ch: 「星核拘束装置」的比例微缩模型，是这颗星球一切灾难的肇因。
t_en: A scale miniature of the Stellaron Imprisonment Device. It housed the reason the planet was plunged into calamity.

t_ch: 放心——它只是一尊模型，应该不会引发你幻听的症状。
t_en: Don't worry, it's just a model. It shouldn't cause you to have any auditory hallucinations... probably.

t_ch: 这件武器被命名为「阿丽萨•兰德的骑枪」，与你手中的那柄别无二致。
t_en: This weapon was named Alisa Rand's Lance, the very same one you hold in your hands.

t_ch: 难道…在那片迷幻空间中与你交流的小女孩就是初代大守护者本人吗？
t_en: Does this mean... that the little girl talking to you in that strange space was the first Supreme Guardian herself?

t_ch: 这件武器被命名为「开拓者的棒球棍」，与你手中的那柄别无二致。
t_en: This weapon was named Trailblazer's Ball Bat, the very same one you hold in your hands.

t_ch: 没想到，你的武器也有被送进博物馆的一天——虽然不是真品，但这也足够震住在它身旁的几位虚卒朋友了。
t_en: You didn't think your weapon would have a place in a museum. This was not the actual weapon, but it was enough to stagger the Voidrangers beside it.

t_ch: 反物质兵团的虚卒冰雕，被八百年前的寒潮冻结至今。
t_en: An ice sculpture of a Voidranger from the Antimatter Legion. It was frozen some eight hundred years ago when the Eternal Freeze arrived.

t_ch: 虚卒的命运通常是在自我毁灭中彻底遭人遗忘。但现它作为展品享受着众人惊奇的目光，或许也算是一种幸运。
t_en: The fate of a Voidranger is to be forgotten during their destruction. This particular one was immortalized as an exhibit, taking in surprised looks of the public, and that might be a form of fortune for its meager existence.

t_ch: 反物质兵团的虚卒冰雕，被八百年前的寒潮冻结至今。
t_en: An ice sculpture of a Voidranger from the Antimatter Legion. It was frozen some eight hundred years ago when the Eternal Freeze arrived.

t_ch: 它很聪明，在被冻住之前摆出了一个帅气的姿势。虽然保不住性命，但保住了面子。
t_en: This is a clever one, as it struck a cool pose before it was frozen solid. It didn't preserve its life, but it did preserve its honor.

t_ch: 反物质兵团的虚卒冰雕，被八百年前的寒潮冻结至今。
t_en: An ice sculpture of a Voidranger from the Antimatter Legion. It was frozen some eight hundred years ago when the Eternal Freeze arrived.

t_ch: 博物馆内如此温暖，你有些担心它们身上的冰是不是已经开始融化了…你说这只是个模型？好吧，那没事了。
t_en: The museum interior is warm, so you were a little worried whether the Voidrangers have started to melt... This is only a model? Oh well, it's nothing.

t_ch: 反物质兵团的虚卒冰雕，被八百年前的寒潮冻结至今。
t_en: An ice sculpture of a Voidranger from the Antimatter Legion. It was frozen some eight hundred years ago when the Eternal Freeze arrived.

t_ch: 虽然你不了解这些虚卒的身体构造，但也许这些怪物现在依然活着，在坚冰下思考脱身的方法……
t_en: You don't understand the physical structure of these Voidrangers, but maybe these monsters are still alive, brainstorming a way to escape inside the solid ice...

t_ch: 一组过去的剪报及其附录。
t_en: A set of newspaper clippings and their appendices.

t_ch: 这篇剪报的标题是「兄弟对簿公堂，歌德家宅暂封」。
t_en: This clipping is titled "Brothers in Court, Goethe Mansion Sealed Temporarily."

t_ch: 一组过去的剪报及其附录。
t_en: A set of newspaper clippings and their appendices.

t_ch: 这篇剪报的标题是「捷音自煦日节晨曦传来，雪原前线我军酣畅大胜」。
t_en: This clipping is titled "Good News at Sunrise for the Solwarm Festival: A Decisive Victory at the Snow Plains War Front."

t_ch: 一组过去的剪报及其附录。
t_en: A set of newspaper clippings and their appendices.

t_ch: 这篇剪报的标题是「大守护者塔提维娜发表重要讲话」。
t_en: This clipping is titled "Supreme Guardian Tatiana Delivers an Important Speech."

t_ch: 一组与大守护者「愚者」希莉儿有关的原始史料。
t_en: A set of historical data about the Supreme Guardian Cyrille the Fool.

t_ch: 有人说，阿基维利的「开拓」之旅是将过去、现在与未来联结的旅途——作为一名星穹列车的开拓客，你出色地完成了这项使命。
t_en: Some say that Akivili's Trailblaze journey is a journey to link the past, the present, and the future. As a Trailblazer aboard the Astral Express, you accomplished this mission with distinction.

t_ch: 长戟与火枪，银鬃铁卫的制式武器。
t_en: Halberds and firearms, the standard Silvermane Guard weapons.

t_ch: 大部分参观者并不知道这些武器的威力——毕竟很少有人能像你一样能有机会实际体验一下。
t_en: Most museumgoers don't know the power of these weapons. After all, few — if any — have had the chance to witness and experience them.

t_ch: 这是一台投影仪。它可以展示博物馆预置的宣讲内容，以便观众更好地了解贝洛伯格的历史文化。
t_en: This is a projector. With it, the museum can project and display preset presentation content to help audience understand the history and culture of Belobog better.

t_ch: 你可以点映自己想看的内容。要看哪个主题好呢？
t_en: You can select and play the content you are interested in. Which topic would you like to select?

t_ch: 主题一：「灾难与星核」。
t_en: Topic 1: "Calamity and Stellaron."

t_ch: 随着一声脆响，有关「灾难与星核」的内容登时出现在博物馆的墙面上，随后是一道沉稳的女声辅以解说。
t_en: With a crisp click, the content regarding "Calamity and Stellaron" appears on the wall of the museum. A velvety female narration accompanies the presentation.

t_ch: …七百年前，来自天外的怪物点燃了星球。生死存亡之际，自天外降临的「星核」响应了阿丽萨•兰德的祈愿，唤来了足以退敌的寒潮。
t_en: ...Seven hundred years ago, monsters from beyond the sky ignited our beautiful planet. In a time of life and death, a Stellaron that descended from outer space heeded Alisa Rand's prayers and summoned the Eternal Freeze to repel our invaders.

t_ch: 然而，「星核」的伟力是恩赐，更是灾难。为了令寒潮退却，无数位大守护者与科研人员前赴后继，矢志究明天外来物的作用原理。
t_en: However, the power of the Stellaron was a blessing but also a curse. In trying to quell the Eternal Freeze, countless Supreme Guardians and researchers devoted their lives to understand how the extraterrestrial object works.

t_ch: 他们不懈为之努力，直到星核终于被彻底抑制——执着的求索者们终于实现了他们的夙愿，进而得以拥抱不见风雪的光明未来。
t_en: They worked ceaselessly until the Stellaron was finally confined — the seekers' persistence finally paid off as we embrace the bright future of a blizzardless world.

t_ch: 解说词到此结束了。
t_en: The narration ends.

t_ch: 主题二：「守护众人之人」。
t_en: Topic 2: "The Protector of All."

t_ch: 随着一声脆响，有关「守护众人之人」的内容登时出现在博物馆的墙面上，随后是一道沉稳的女声辅以解说。
t_en: With a crisp click, the content regarding "The Protector of All" appears on the wall of the museum. A velvety female narration accompanies the presentation.

t_ch: …自贝洛伯格建城至今，一任又一任的大守护者承「琥珀王」克里珀恩赐，为众人带去祂的庇护，引领众人向前。
t_en: ...Since Belobog's foundation, each Supreme Guardian received the blessing of the Amber Lord, leading the citizens to move ahead under THEIR protection.

t_ch: 第十八任大守护者可可利亚与天外来客并肩作战，最终以她自己的生命为代价，彻底终结了肆虐近千年的风雪——他们无愧为贝洛伯格的铁壁。
t_en: The eighteenth Supreme Guardian, Madam Cocolia did battle alongside allies from beyond the sky, and ended up sacrificing herself to eradicate this blizzard that ravaged our planet for nearly a thousand years. They are, undeniably, the iron walls of Belobog.

t_ch: 然而，在银鬃铁卫奋勇作战的同时，人们也始终未曾放弃挣扎——在高墙之底、岩盘之下，贝洛伯格的子民们于黑暗中紧握希望，终于迎来自由的天地。
t_en: However, whilst the Silvermane Guards battled for our safety, the citizens of Belobog never gave up on their own struggles — under the tall wall, beneath the stone disk, Belobogians held on tightly to hope in the darkness, and finally welcomed a future of freedom.

t_ch: 解说词到此结束了。
t_en: The narration ends.

t_ch: 主题三：「仰望星辰」。
t_en: Topic 3: Gaze Upon the Stars

t_ch: 随着一声脆响，有关「仰望星辰」的内容登时出现在博物馆的墙面上，随后是一道沉稳的女声辅以解说。
t_en: With a crisp click, the content regarding "Gaze Upon the Stars" appears on the wall of the museum. A velvety female narration accompanies the presentation.

t_ch: …可以说，若没有「开拓者三人组」的协助，星核便不可能得到抑制。他们乘坐神秘的列车自星穹降临贝洛伯格——这一次，天外来客没有带来战火，而是未来。
t_en: ...It can be said that, without the help of the Trailblaze Trio, the Stellaron wouldn't be contained so successfully. They arrived at Belobog on a mysterious train from the stars. This time, visitors from outer space didn't bring forth the smog of war, but the light of hope.

t_ch: 他们凭借自身的本领，为这座城市重拾了希望——在他们的帮助下，贝洛伯格的居民这才重新得见晴朗的天空，这才得以重新走进无际雪原，探索全新的天地。
t_en: With their own abilities, they brought solace and aspiration back to the city. With their help, Belobogians were finally able to see the clear skies once again, and were inspired to revisit the boundless snow plains to explore a whole new world.

t_ch: 他们无私奉献，不求回报。在解决了星核危机后，他们便又重新踏上旅途，随星穹列车一道化作象征祈愿与希冀的流星。
t_en: They gave selflessly, not asking for anything in return. After resolving the Stellaron Crisis, they returned to their journey, becoming shooting stars symbolizing blessings and hope aboard the cavalcade from outer space.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}、三月七、丹恒，这就是他们的名字。若你时常想念为众人带来拯救的开拓者们，不妨抬头仰望——他们或许正在星海间畅游，而冬城的希望也在彼处。
t_en: Their names were {NICKNAME}, March 7th, and Dan Heng. If you often find yourself missing the Trailblazers who brought salvation, look up and gaze. They might be traveling among the stars, with wishes of the Everwinter City by their side.

t_ch: 解说词到此结束了。
t_en: The narration ends.

t_ch: 先不看了。
t_en: I'll pass.

t_ch: 你可以随时回到这里，点映想看的内容。
t_en: You can always come back and review the content you would like to see.

t_ch: （这应该就是照片上的物件了…把它带回去吧。）
t_en: (This should be the item in the photo... I'll bring it back.)

t_ch: 你将展品带回了贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆……
t_en: You've brought the exhibit back to the Belobog History and Culture Museum...

t_ch: 博物馆的员工将展品放在了正确的位置……
t_en: The museum staff have placed the exhibit in the correct position...

t_ch: （看来这件展品也可以顺利展出了……）
t_en: (Looks like this piece can now be put on display...)

t_ch: 幽灵星槎？我很久之前就听人说过类似的「都市传说」……
t_en: A phantom starskiff? I heard of a similar "urban legend" long ago...

t_ch: 传说在迴星港中，有两位监船工人在巡逻检视培养舱，一艘幽灵星槎缓缓驶向他们，无声无息，就像是从虚空来访的使者。
t_en: Legend says that at the Stargazer Navalia, a phantom starskiff had gradually and silently approached two workers when they were patrolling among the incubators. The skiff was as quiet as a messenger visiting from the void beyond.

t_ch: 胆子大的监船工人登上星槎查看，发现船上空无一人，只有呜呜哀嚎的学舌妖，走向深处后，他发现船上货物满载，甚至还有散发着热气的食物。
t_en: The more courageous worker boarded the starskiff to take a look, only to discover that there was no one on the skiff. There was only a wailing Parrot Goblin. As he walked deeper, he found the skiff full of cargo, and even contained food that was still warm.

t_ch: 藿藿，你、你别怕……
t_en: Huohuo, d—don't be afraid...

t_ch: 素裳小姐才是，你好像在发抖……
t_en: But Miss Sushang, you seem to be the one trembling...

t_ch: 唯一的诡秘之处在于甲板上满是血污，像是发生过什么争斗…他进一步检视了航线图，发现这艘星槎来自岱舆仙舟，去往苍城仙舟。
t_en: The only mystery was that the deck was covered in blood, as if there was a rough battle before... The worker examined the route map and found out that this starskiff originated from the Xianzhou Daiyu and was heading toward the Xianzhou Cangcheng.

t_ch: 可是…这两艘仙舟不都……
t_en: But... Aren't these two Xianzhous...

t_ch: 失去方向的星槎，迷航了……
t_en: Looks like the starskiff had lost her direction and gone off course...

t_ch: 胆小的监船工人像是听到了同伴呼唤，亦步亦趋地登上了幽灵星槎…随后星槎像雾气般缓缓消散在迴星港里。从此再没人见过这两位工人了。
t_en: The timid worker thought he heard his partner calling and boarded the phantom starskiff... Soon after, the starskiff slowly faded from Stargazer Navalia. From then on, no one had ever seen the two workers again.

t_ch: 呜呜啊啊……
t_en: Woaaahhh...

t_ch: 藿藿——
t_en: Huohuo...

t_ch: 有点意思，还有后续吗？
t_en: Interesting. Is there any more?

t_ch: 你要是喜欢，还可以有后续。
t_en: If you like it, there's a follow-up story.

t_ch: 没有人逃出来，前面都是你编的？
t_en: Since no one manages to escape, did you make up the story?

t_ch: 家人，看破不说破嘛。
t_en: C'mon fam, don't point it out like this.

t_ch: 既然有人目击到了幽灵船，这很有可能是岁阳作祟，我们肯定要去调查的，不是吗？
t_en: Since someone has witnessed the ghost ship, it may be a heliobus' doing. We are going to investigate it, no?

t_ch: 幽灵星槎也是岁阳造成的吗？
t_en: Can a heliobus create a phantom starskiff too?

t_ch: …我不太确定，不过很有可能！
t_en: ...I'm not sure, but it's highly possible!

t_ch: 仙舟上许多年前失踪过船只吗？
t_en: Was a missing ship reported from Xianzhou many years ago?

t_ch: 据我所知，有不少哦！
t_en: From what I know, there were many!

t_ch: …只、只要我们不登上星槎肯定没事的，素裳小姐。
t_en: ...A—As long as we don't board the starskiff, we should be fine, Miss Sushang.

t_ch: 走、走吧，捉鬼小队出动！
t_en: L—Let's go. Ghost-hunting squad, move out!

t_ch: 出动！
t_en: Move out!

t_ch: 发帖人说是在这附近见到了幽灵星槎。不过嘛，既然是幽灵船，肯定不会被轻易找到。要不，我们就在这里分头行动，找到线索后再集合？
t_en: The post said they saw the phantom starskiff around here. But since it's a phantom starskiff, I doubt we can spot it easily. How about we split our ways here and regroup once we find clues?

t_ch: 恐怖片里最忌讳分头行动了。
t_en: Splitting up is taboo in horror movies.

t_ch: 对呀对呀。
t_en: Yeah, yeah.

t_ch: 我们还是一起行动吧！
t_en: It's better for us to go together!

t_ch: 我赞成。
t_en: I agree.

t_ch: 我反对。
t_en: I disagree.

t_ch: …我也反对，两票对两票。
t_en: ...I disagree too. Two against two.

t_ch: …你们也太谨慎了吧，那好吧，我们一起去找找吧。
t_en: ...You all are overcautious. Fine, let's search together.

t_ch: （就在这附近找找幽灵星槎的线索吧……）
t_en: (Search the clues of the phantom starskiff nearby...)

t_ch: （难道说，那就是传闻中的幽灵星槎？）
t_en: (Could that be the legendary phantom starskiff?)

t_ch: （再靠近些看看吧。）
t_en: (Let's get closer and take a look.)

t_ch: 驭空…你会来吗……
t_en: Yukong... Will you come...

t_ch: 驭空…我们需要聊聊……
t_en: Yukong... We need to talk...

t_ch: 这么没头脑地找也缺乏效率，不如我们找个高点儿的地方？
t_en: We're not gonna be efficient running around like headless chickens. Why don't we get to the high ground?

t_ch: （幽灵星槎好像消失了……）
t_en: (The phantom starskiff disappeared...)

t_ch: （那里好像有个人，去问问看吧……）
t_en: (It seems like there's someone there. I'll try asking them...)

t_ch: …消失了！你们看到了吗？幽灵星槎消失了！
t_en: ...It disappeared! Did you see it? The phantom starskiff disappeared!

t_ch: …请问您是？
t_en: ...Could I ask who you are?

t_ch: 我叫作晴霓，之前和{NICKNAME}打过照面。
t_en: I'm Qingni. I've met {NICKNAME} before.

t_ch: 我叫作晴霓，是天舶司的职员。
t_en: I'm Qingni, a staff member from the Sky-Faring Commission.

t_ch: …天舶司也在寻找幽灵星槎吗？
t_en: ...Is the Sky-Faring Commission looking for the phantom starskiff too?

t_ch: 嘘——这是我擅自行动的，我家那位司舵大人可不允许我追查什么幽灵星槎。
t_en: Shh — I acted on my own. Madam Helm Master won't allow me to track down anything like a phantom starskiff.

t_ch: 她也不允许其他人去找幽灵星槎——说什么「不过是些怪力乱神，讨论它们只是在浪费时间」。
t_en: She doesn't allow the others to investigate it either... To her, it's "just some supernatural stuff, and it's a waste of time to discuss it."

t_ch: 但是，你看到了网上的帖子。
t_en: But you saw the post online.

t_ch: 没错，我就想来这里找找看。
t_en: That's right, and I thought of coming here to try to find it.

t_ch: 结果就是，你看到了，我也看到了。但它又消失了。
t_en: In the end, you saw it, and I saw it too. But it disappeared.

t_ch: 多好的机会呀！我还以为我能登上去拍个视频呢…这下可好，白白错失了一个爆火新闻的机会。
t_en: What a pity! I thought I could board it and film a video... And now I've missed the opportunity to make a hit post.

t_ch: 登上去？你怎么会这么想，万一遇到什么怪事可怎么办？
t_en: Board it? Why would you do that? What if something strange happened to you?

t_ch: 晴霓小姐就是为了打探这个才来的吗？
t_en: Miss Qingni, are you here to find out more about the skiff?

t_ch: …是的。我听人说起，我妈…呃，也就是驭空大人在几日前登上过这艘星槎。
t_en: ...Yes. I heard someone said my mother... Uhm, Madam Yukong had boarded the starskiff a few days ago.

t_ch: 也许是我疑神疑鬼了。这几天她变得和平时有些不一样，总是长吁短叹、多愁善感…就像是变了个人。
t_en: Perhaps I'm being paranoid, but I do find my mother different from her usual self. She often moans and sighs, seemingly moody all day... It's as if she's become another person.

t_ch: 那个从来都很果决硬气的妈妈，怎么会变成这样呢？所以我搜集了几条关于这艘星槎的传言，来到这儿，想探个究竟。
t_en: Why would someone as unwavering and firm like my Mom turn out like this? So, I've researched several rumors about this starskiff, and here I am to investigate it further.

t_ch: 那可能就是被附身了。
t_en: Perhaps she is being possessed.

t_ch: 什、什么意思？
t_en: Wh—What do you mean?

t_ch: 她竟然登上过幽灵星槎？
t_en: She actually boarded the phantom starskiff?

t_ch: 这也是别人告诉我的，我问过她，她不承认。
t_en: I heard that from others. I asked her, but she denied it.

t_ch: …这就像是被岁阳附身了一样。
t_en: ...Sounds like she is being possessed by a heliobus.
